payload.review_text: Plan-safety audit iteration 1 of PLAN-AA0. 0 blockers — mechanically executable as assigned (sonnet). Substrate fidelity: all named entities (_add_run_args, _run, load_config, _apply_overrides, create_session_dir, config.paths.output_dir, cli.py:30, config.py:125-126, session.py:29) verified against declared substrate; find_completed_session is Step 3's deliverable. Step 7 CLAUDE.md edit permitted — CLAUDE.md at project root, not under bundle-managed dirs; Diarizer-CLI bullet is above the plan-foundry sentinel; step's CRITICAL note correctly bounds the edit. Falsifiability: no vacuous assertions. Step count 7 (<12). Non-blockers: (H204) Step 3 parenthetical '(re, pathlib - already imported)' ambiguous, re not currently in session.py; (PPV004) verify: test -f Transcribe.cmd POSIX builtin unannotated but runnable via Bash tool, no formal Windows CI so no escalation; (H003) Step 4 variable 'existing' assignment implicit; (H003) Step 5 exact multi-file note text unspecified. All non-blocking; carried to executor as advisories. Ready to advance to checked.

findings.message: Parenthetical '(re, pathlib - already imported)' is ambiguous; re is not currently imported in diarizer/session.py. Executor may omit import re, causing NameError caught at acceptance time.
findings.suggested_fix: State 'add import re at top of file; from pathlib import Path already present.'

findings.message: verify: test -f Transcribe.cmd uses POSIX test -f without # platform annotation. Runnable via Bash tool on this Windows project; no formal CI so no escalation to blocker.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite as python -c pathlib check or annotate # platform: posix.

findings.message: Variable 'existing' used in f-string but assignment not explicitly declared; implied. Sonnet executor would infer.
Exact text of the multi-file note not specified (usage message is quoted). Executor invents reasonable text.
findings.suggested_fix: Add literal string e.g. 'Note: only the first dropped file is processed.'
